articles.title: Ukrainian drones hit more sites of Russian online retailer Wildberries
articles.summary: Logistics hubs belonging to Wildberries in the Krasnodar and Stavropol regions were struck overnight.

articles.title: Zelensky sacks Ukraine's top army commander after days of protests

articles.summary: The hearing was disrupted by protests and a shouting match as Hegseth testified at a pivotal time in the war.

articles.title: India's Modi government under growing pressure as 'cockroach' protests intensify
articles.summary: The CJP got a boost on Tuesday when the opposition Congress party demonstrated outside the PM's house.

articles.title: Modelling scout linked to Epstein found dead in Paris suburbs
articles.summary: Daniel Siad, whose name appears in the Epstein files thousands of times, had previously denied knowledge of the threat Jeffrey Epstein posed.

articles.title: Tokyo urges men to wear shorts to work, but some women say it's 'leg hair harassment'
articles.summary: The Tokyo Metropolitan Government wants workers to ditch the suit and tie for t-shirts, trainers - and shorts.

articles.title: Police station opens at Hitler's birthplace in bid to rid site of Nazi link
articles.summary: The opening follows years of controversy about what to do with the 17th-Century former inn, where the Nazi dictator was born.

articles.title: US renews strikes on Iran as Trump threatens to attack underground nuclear site
articles.summary: The US president's threat to target a key Iranian nuclear site came ahead of the 11th consecutive night of attacks.

articles.title: France passes law banning under-15s from social media
articles.summary: The law will require social media platforms in France to verify all users age from January.

articles.title: Michelin-starred restaurateur faces jail for using ants as dessert garnish
articles.summary: Officials say the restaurant in South Korea used some 49,000 ants in various dishes over four years.
